channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Polymarket and Kalshi’s CEOs Hate Each Other
These States Are Joining in the Push to Ban Surveillance Pricing
item.description: At least a dozen states are considering legislation.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Sunday, March 8 (game #1001)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Sunday, March 8 (game #735)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: Leading by example: Embracing tools internally before shipping them externally
item.description: Using your own tools builds trust, improves quality, accelerates innovation, and protects customers from failure.

item.title: Cet écran tire le meilleur de votre vidéoprojecteur : j’ai testé le Nothing Projector ALR Longue focale
item.description: L’écran Nothing Projector ALR Longue focale promet de transformer un simple vidéoprojecteur en véritable installation home cinéma, même dans une pièce de vie. Pensé pour les projecteurs longue focale et standard, en voici nos impressions.

item.title: Wrexham vs Chelsea Free Streams: How to Watch FA Cup 5th Round Tie 2025/26 Online From Anywhere
item.description: All the ways to watch Wrexham vs Chelsea in the FA Cup fifth round, with the Welsh club seeking a shock victory over the Premier League giants.

item.title: Deux smartphones notés 8/10 à prix cassé : faut-il choisir Xiaomi ou Honor ?
item.description: Le Xiaomi Redmi Note 15 Pro 5G et le Honor Magic 8 Lite : deux pépites notées 8/10 actuellement en promotion aux alentours de 300 euros ! Ils comptent parmi les smartphones les plus équilibrés du moment, mais lequel de ces deux modèles mérite votre attention ?

item.title: Engadget review recap: Galaxy S26 Ultra, Galaxy Buds 4, Dell XPS 14 and more
item.description:
It’s a busy time for the reviews team and Engadget, and with Apple announcing new devices this week, we aren’t letting up any time soon. New products from Samsung, Dell, Google and ASUS headline the roundup this time, and we’ve got a few unique items to discuss as well. Read on to catch up on anything you might’ve missed, including the latest installment of Pokémon. Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra
This year’s Samsung flagship phone may not impress you with a load of new features, but there’s one in particular that senior reporter Sam Rutherford was wowed by. “This goes double for the S26 Ultra, who...

item.title: The OpenClaw superfan meetup serves optimism and lobster
item.description: The woman at the door wore a plush lobster headdress. She sat in the front hallway of a multistory event venue in Manhattan, beside a bundle of wristbands. If she granted you one, the world of ClawCon beckoned behind her - full of vibey pink and purple lighting, lobster claw headbands, multicolored name tags, sponsor […]

item.title: A bite-sized adventure that puts a wrench into the classic Zelda formula
item.description: There are a lot of games that try to emulate The Legend of Zelda, but few that manage to capture that spirit in such a small, concise package as Ratcheteer DX. The postapocalyptic game only takes a few hours to complete, but over that span it nails the classic Zelda vibe almost perfectly, offering a […]

item.title: I watched the first trailer for Lanterns multiple times — these are the 7 biggest questions I have about the new HBO Max show
item.description: After HBO and DC Studios debuted Lanterns' first teaser, these are the things I'm most curious about.

item.title: Dell XPS 14 (2026) review: A beautiful laptop that excels at almost everything… except typing
item.description: There is so much to love about Dell's new XPS 14, it's hard for me to discuss it without sounding like a total fanboy. Its new design makes it incredibly thin and light. And Intel's Panther Lake chips give it a ton of power, even when it comes to games. After reviving the XPS brand from last year's ignominious rebranding, it's as if Dell was laser-focused on fixing all the issues we've had with the XPS 14 so far. Unfortunately, a shallow, unresponsive and hilariously error-prone keyboard kept me from truly loving the XPS 14. Simply put, it has trouble recognizing keys if you press them too qui...

item.title: “We intend to launch our first end-to-end commercial DNA Data Storage solutions before the end of 2026”: Biomemory outlines its DNA storage roadmap after Catalog deal
item.description: French DNA storage pioneer Biomemory has acquired rival Catalog, and told us where it sees the technology in the years ahead.
